recalls.Description: Taylor Water Technologies is recalling Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles included in the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits #K-1106, as well as those sold separately. These kits are designed for testing phosphate levels in water for pools, spas, and aquariums. The affected bottles contain sulfuric acid and are labeled as Phosphate Reagent #1, with part number R-0980 and a volume of 22 mL (.75 oz). The shelf-life of Phosphate Reagent #1 is 24 months.
recalls.Title: Taylor Water Technologies Recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles Due to Chemical Burn Risk and Non-Compliant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Taylor Water Technologies at 800-837-8548 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit their website at https://www.taylortechnologies.com for more information on the recall.

Products.Name: Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles from Taylor Phosphate Test Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle sold within the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits
Taylor Phosphate Test Kit with recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Taylor Water Technologies LLC, of Sparks, Maryland

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.taylortechnologies.com and in pool and aquarium retail stores across the country from August 2022 through August 2024, priced around $13 for the reagent bottles and $56 for the complete kits.

Hazards.Name: Risk of chemical burns due to sulfuric acid in non-child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles, secure them away from children, and contact Taylor Water Technologies for disposal instructions and a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Taylor Water Technologies recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles due to chemical burn risk and non-compliance with safety packaging regulations.

recalls.Description:
Torquay eTrading LLC is recalling Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip & Rip re-gripping kits due to a potential poisoning hazard. These products, packaged in 8-ounce white bottles with blue labels, were marketed with misleading safety claims, stating they are 'Safe & Non-Toxic' without the necessary cautionary warnings for petroleum distillates. This poses a significant risk to consumers, especially children.
The recalled items were available individually or in multi-packs, including 2-pack, 4-pack, 6-pack, and a bundle with 28 golf strips. Consumers are urged to stop using these products immediately and reach out to Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label to ensure safe usage.
recalls.URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
recalls.Title: Recall of Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@essentialvalues.us. Additional information can be found online at https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall or by visiting https://www.essentialvalues.us and clicking 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 through May 2024, priced around $9, with kits including golf grip tape priced at approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading safety labeling

Remedies.Name: Stop using the products and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label

recalls.metaDescription: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits recalled due to poisoning risk. Contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label.

recalls.Description: Wet & Forget is recalling its "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover, available in 48 oz. and 68 oz. bottles with hose end nozzles. The product, packaged in a blue plastic bottle, has UPCs 879288000077 for the 48 oz. size and 879288000084 for the 68 oz. size, located on the label's bottom right corner. The hose end nozzle is marked with the "Wet & Forget" brand name.
recalls.Title: Wet & Forget USA Issues Recall for 2.7 Million Bottles of "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover Due to Skin and Eye Irritation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled product and reach out to Wet & Forget at 888-359-4623 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or via email at nozzle@realtimeresults.net. More information is available online at www.recallrtr.com/nozzle under the "Safety Recalls" section.

Products.Name: Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Images.Caption: Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 48 oz. Bottle
Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 68 oz. bottle
Recalled hose-end nozzle sold with Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 3,188 reports of dislodgement of the nozzle insert, including 157 reports of exposure to the cleaning solution and 28 reports that include complaints of skin or eye irritation.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ace Hardware, Costco, Home Depot, Lowe's, True Value, Walmart, and various home goods stores nationwide, as well as online at www.amazon.com, www.SamsClubs.com, www.walmart.com from February 2018 through November 2023, priced between $30 and $35.

Importers.Name: Wet & Forget (USA) Inc., of Elgin, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of skin and eye irritation due to dislodging hose end nozzle clip

Remedies.Name: Free replacement nozzle for the recalled stain remover; do not use until replacement is installed

recalls.metaDescription: Wet & Forget recalls 2.7 million bottles of "Xtreme Reach" stain remover due to skin and eye irritation risk. Learn more about the recall.
